§6Reactor Grade Plutonium§f

§aMixture of Pu-239 and Pu-240 — the standard form of plutonium for reactor fuel!§f

§bReactor Grade Plutonium§f (RGPu) is a mixture of §e66.6%§f Pu-239 (§e6§f nuggets) and §e33.3%§f Pu-240 (§e3§f nuggets). It's a natural product of raw plutonium enrichment.

§6Properties§f:
§b• Radioactivity§f: §e6.25§f RAD/s
§b• Category§f: Fuel mixture

§6Production§f:
§b• Plutonium Hexafluoride§f enrichment (Gas Centrifuge)
§b• Byproduct of§f uranium fuel recycling
§b• Breeding U-238§f in a Chicago Pile
§b• Recycling§f: plutonium and MOX fuel reprocessing
§b• Crafting§f: §e6×§f Pu-239 nuggets + §e3×§f Pu-240 nuggets (shapeless)

§6Uses§f:
§b• Plutonium Fuel§f: standard reactor fuel production
§b• MOX Fuel§f: mixed oxide fuel production
§b• SILEX Separation§f: further refine into pure Pu-239 and Pu-240 for weapons production

§eTip§f: Reactor Grade Plutonium is fine for fuel but not for weapons (due to Pu-240 content). Use the §bSILEX§f to separate RGP into weapons-grade Pu-239 for nuclear warheads!
